text:
Once upon a moonlit night, young Olive was lost. Lost in a forest. Just any ordinary night. Just any.

She wandered aimlessly; squirrels chattered, birds piped... and then something odd happened.

She heard a voice.

Not just any voice... it was her Aunt Penelope's cheerful complaining.

Olive froze. "What on earth?"

Aunt Penelope was somewhere in that forest.

"How delightful." Her voice rang out with sickening delight. Olive braced herself.

"You're just as peculiar now as always. You should turn into a tree."

The voice chuckled. That wasn't just Aunt Penelope.

As Olive pursued the chuckles...

...she stumbled upon a clearing.

There was a tall woman holding a gnarled book.

"Astounding."

Then?

The book flew open.

Inside was a atlas.

Olive stood gaping.

On one corner was a map.

Just a map.

A map to nowhere.

Olive tugged.

Nothing. Just
